steps.title: Pin down the main purpose using the final sentence
steps.content:
The passage describes A.D. Carson’s album as “the first peer-reviewed rap album,” details its unusual dual review by scholars and rap artists, and closes by saying it “helped redefine how scholarship is created and shared.” Every detail supports one goal: showing why this album is uniquely innovative.
  • Choice A — ‘To compare the relative public impact of scholarly articles and albums’: The passage never ranks or compares the societal reach of articles versus albums. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘To capture one scholar’s opinion of a new rap album’: No individual scholar’s opinion is quoted or presented; the passage is descriptive, not evaluative. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To explain why a certain rap album is particularly innovative’: Every detail — first peer-reviewed rap album, dual expert review, mixtape-plus-essay hybrid format — supports the argument that the album is genuinely novel. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘To describe how each step of the peer review process unfolds’: The passage only briefly mentions peer review; it does not walk through its steps. ✗

steps.title: Confirm with the closing sentence
steps.content: The passage ends with the album having “helped redefine how scholarship is created and shared,” which frames the entire text as an explanation of the album’s innovation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
